pubmed_links: [Harripaul et al. (2017) Mapping autosomal recessive intellectual disability: combined microarray and exome sequencing identifies 26 novel candidate genes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28397838/); [Komulainen et al. (2014) JNK1 controls dendritic field size in L2/3 and L5 of the motor cortex, constrains soma size, and influences fine motor coordination](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25309320/); [Morel et al. (2018) JIP1-mediated JNK activation negatively regulates synaptic plasticity and spatial memory](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29540552/)
summary: MAPK8 (JNK1) encodes a kinase whose signaling pathway has been genetically implicated in autism spectrum disorder, schizophrenia, and intellectual disability; loss-of-function variants in MAPK8 were identified in consanguineous families with autosomal recessive intellectual disability, and JNK1 dysregulation disrupts synaptic plasticity and dendritic architecture relevant to neurodevelopmental phenotypes. (emerging evidence)
